channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: My idle ClickHouse was merging 11 million rows every 30 seconds
item.description: I run a small self-hosted observability tool on the cheapest VPS I could find on purpose: 2 cores, 2 GB RAM, 20 GB SATA SSD. It ingests errors, traces and metrics from two low-traffic sites of...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Illex Squid Catch in the Falklands
item.description: Lower catch this year. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

item.title: Rod Johnson Is Back - and He's Bringing AI Agents to Java
item.description: If you have written enterprise Java in the last 20 years, you know the name Rod Johnson. He created Spring Framework back in 2003 - the thing that made Java dependency injection feel natural...

item.title: Deploying Rails 8 on Render Free Tier: Bypassing the 512MB RAM and Read-Only Storage Limits
item.description: 1. Introduction Hello from Japan! 🇯🇵 I am an active truck driver in Japan self-studying Python, leveraging my logistics domain knowledge to become a Web Engineer. (English is my second language,...

item.title: I Audited 12 Open Source JWT Implementations and Found the Same 6 Mistakes
item.description: I spent last month reviewing JWT implementations across 12 open-source Node.js projects on GitHub — ranging from starter templates with 2k stars to production boilerplates used by teams at real...

item.title: I built Commitea: Git and GitHub explained in Spanish, with an interactive visualizer
item.description: When I started programming, Git was hard for me — like anything new is at first. Over time I noticed something: most of the best resources for learning it are in English. And for a lot of people...
Today I played around with CiscoNet (this is just what I'm gonna call it, it's easier) module 4 and did my first Packet Tracer exercise. That took most of the afternoon and made me very tired. I...

item.title: The Million-Tenant Problem: Why Your OpenSearch Service Architecture Breaks at Scale
item.description: Almost every search application is multi-tenant, whether or not anyone called it that. A store has product categories. A SaaS product has customers. A logging platform has teams, an e-commerce...

item.title: Your Search Engine Has Been Doing the Heavy Lifting (And You Never Noticed)
item.description: You type a query into a box. Three milliseconds later the thing you wanted is sitting at the top of the results, and it feels like the machine read your mind. It did not. Your words got torn into...

item.title: A skill nobody reaches for is dead weight
item.description: Same task. Two ways of asking. Two very different results. That was the moment it clicked for me, and it has bugged me ever since. I gave my AI coding agent something to do, got back work that was...

item.title: Left of the Loop: The Hestia
item.description: A hearth fire required no priest, no temple, no calendar date. It required a fire and a household. When a city sent settlers to found a colony, they didn’t design a new fire from scratch. They...

item.title: Meta just created a moderation nightmare for its smart glasses
item.description: Meta's smart glasses have been a PR headache for the company. Public backlash has been swift, and fierce; people are concerned about the erosion of privacy and expansion of surveillance. Some...

item.title: Qualcomm is about to raise prices and that’s bad news for everyone
item.description: Qualcomm sent a letter to customers on Friday warning of plans to increase its prices by "a percentage in the double digits," Bloomberg reports. The price hikes will go into effect starting with...

item.title: Midjourney bought the astrology app Co-Star
item.description: Midjourney, which has gone from generating AI cat images to full-body ultrasound scans, is getting into a new field: astrology. The AI startup announced on Thursday that it has acquired the...

item.title: DJI camera clone company Xtra is halting and refunding all preorders
item.description: After selling a barely disguised version of the hit DJI Osmo Pocket 3 in the United States last year, Xtra Technology seemed ready to sneak in its version of the new dual-lens Osmo Pocket 4 Pro,...

item.title: Nothing confirms layoffs, but calls market exit rumors ‘fake news’
item.description: In response to a report that Nothing is planning to "exit 12 markets as global shipments decline," Nothing cofounder Akis Evangelidis said the company is "reorganizing" and laying off some of its...

item.title: Why Cognition bought Poke: AI personality is becoming a competitive advantage
item.description: AI coding startup Cognition has acquired Poke, the AI assistant you text like a friend, in a deal valuing the startup in the low nine figures. The acquisition brings Poke’s conversational style...

item.title: Roku raises streaming hardware prices by up to $50
item.description: Roku has increased prices across its streaming hardware, with the cheapest HD Streaming Stick now priced at $39.99 instead of $29.99, as first reported by The Desk. The price hike affects all of...

item.title: Did Chinese AI Steal From Anthropic, and OpenAI Loses Control of Two Models
item.description: On this episode of Uncanny Valley, we dive into accusations that China’s Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, and how the US Army needs to cut back on AI use.

item.title: Blade Runner 2099’s moody dystopia streams on Amazon in November
item.description: After teasing the series with some first-look images yesterday, Amazon is finally properly unveiling its Blade Runner streaming series. Called Blade Runner 2099, the show hits Prime Video on...

item.title: You can’t ignore Google Zero anymore
item.description: The web and Google once had a deal: Google collects data and indexes webpages and in exchange sends oceans of traffic to websites. The deal wasn't perfect and certainly made Google more money than...

item.title: The quest to keep organs alive outside the body
item.description: This week, I covered a fascinating effort to preserve organs outside the body. There’s a huge shortage of donor organs, and one of the main reasons is time—they survive only a matter of hours...

item.title: Anthropic launches Opus 5
item.description: Opus 5 will be both cheaper and less restrictive than Fable, likely making it preferable in most use cases.

item.title: Anthropic releases Opus 5 with ‘close’ to Fable 5’s capabilities
item.description: Weeks after Anthropic's latest toe-to-toe with the US government, and days after an OpenAI security incident that dominated tech industry discussions, Anthropic on Thursday released its newest...

item.title: Meta is making its AI chatbot more like an assistant
item.description: Meta is upgrading its AI chatbot with new productivity features in a bid to compete with rivals like Gemini, ChatGPT, and Claude. The update will allow Meta AI to tap into your calendar to help...

item.title: As US weighs response to Chinese AI, industry urges against broad open-weight restrictions
item.description: AI companies, including Nvidia and Mistral, urge policymakers to avoid broad restrictions on open-weight AI models as Washington debates responses to Chinese AI and alleged model distillation.

item.title: Bluesky’s AI assistant Attie expands into an open social research tool
item.description: Users can now ask Attie questions about news, trends, and conversations on Bluesky and other apps on the AT Protocol.

item.title: BlueNoroff Zoom Phishing Kit Profiles Crypto Wallets Before Malware Delivery
item.description: The North Korean threat actors behind the ClickFix-style campaigns that employ typosquatted Zoom and Microsoft Teams domains have been found to operate an active phishing kit to impersonate the...

item.title: Midjourney acquired the astrology app Co-Star
item.description: The AI lab Midjourney continues to expand its purview beyond image and video generation.

item.title: Silicon Valley Is Completely Divided Over Chinese AI
item.description: The AI “startups” worth billions of dollars are raising alarm bells about Chinese AI. The smaller players have a totally different take.
